rulings.Case Title: ARAFAT HERZALLAH VS. NIMER MASSIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Notice
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 24, 2019. DEFENDANTS DONNA STERN, JOE COLEMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Notice Hearing Required. By no later than 9:00 a.m. on January 25, 2019 Plaintiff to submit a brief in the format of a separate statement including each fact (either from Defendant's Separate Statement or from Plaintiff's set of Additional Facts; the numbering of the facts shall be the same as in the previously submitted separate statements) listing all facts (and supporting evidence) showing that the specific acts of Coleman and Stern were "wrongful in their nature" and not within the scope of employment, e.g. review proposed transferee's credit report vs. intentionally omit valuable assets of the transferee from the evaluation of tenant fitness. The Court is inclined to GRANT the motion. Moving party shifted its burden. Opposing party failed to create a material issue of triable facts regarding any acts of the employees that were wrongful in nature and outside of the ordinary course of their work duties. The Court notes that the response separate statement prepared by the opposing party was in an improper format. Plaintiff's counsel must comply with CCP 437c and related Rules of Court in the future. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN DORGAN VS. EDWARD CHUTE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Chute For Cancellation Of Time-Barred Lien
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 24, 2019. DEFENDANT EDMUND CHUTE'S Motion Of Chute For Cancellation Of Time-Barred Lien DENIED. "If a claims has not yet sued to foreclose [mechanic's lien], and owner's speediest remedy is through proceedings for injunctive or declaratory relief." If and when claimant brings an action to foreclosure the lien, the owner may more easily file a motion in the pending action. Lambert v. Superior Court (1991) 228 Cal.App.3d 383. There is no pending action to foreclose the lien before this Court. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: OLUSOJI FANOIKI VS. 1081-1087 MISSISSIPPI STREET ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Mpa In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion For Preliminary Injunction To Prohibit Foreclosure Sale
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 24, 2019. PLAINTIFF OLUSOJI FANOIKI'S Notice And Mpa In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion For Preliminary Injunction To Prohibit Foreclosure Sale continued to February 8, 2019. By no later than January 31, 2019 the parties are to submit a joint brief delineating (preferably in a table format) the total amount of the assessment/fines subject to potential foreclosure; the specific amounts of each assessment/fine and the date; authority per CC&R; the date when the assessment/fine was approved, e.g. date of the meeting where a vote took place; the date the demand for payment of each amount was given to Defendant; any other amount/date information that parties consider relevant. If Plaintiff disputes the cited authority per CC&R or the fact of the vote (not the propriety of it), Defendant may include a separate column to so state. Sample Table 1. Assessment/Fine Amount; 2. Date approved; 3. Date payment demanded; 4. CC&R section, etc. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: OLUSOJI FANOIKI VS. 1081-1087 MISSISSIPPI STREET ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint (Code Civ. Proc. 425.16)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 24, 2019. DEFENDANTS BRIAN REED, 1081-1087 MISSISSIPPI STREET HOMEOWNERS' Motion And Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint (Code Civ. Proc. 425.16) DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burden and establish that any claim advanced in Plaintiff's complaint arises out of protected activity. Each of Plaintiff's claims specifically states the basis (e.g. paragraph 41) and does not arise out of any "oral or written statements." Furthermore, Defendant's employ an improper analysis of the applicability of Anti-SLAPP. Specifically, Defendant references "the gravamen of the complaint," a concept abandoned since Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376. While Defendant relies on Lee v. Silveira (2016) 6 Cal.App.5th 527, Damon v. Ocean Hills Journalist Club (2000) 85 Cal.app.4th 468, and Schroeder v. Irvine City Council (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 174 Defendant failed to identify a single claim in the complaint that arises out of the content of the vote, or statements made during the meetings, etc. = (501/CFH)
